purpose: Evidence-first breast pathology sign-out training for pathology residents, with case reasoning, report construction, and image-governed remediation.

externalImplementationPatterns.implementationUse: Use for high-resolution histology, whole-slide regions, and learner pan/zoom review before diagnosis reveal.
Use for extracting figures, tables, or case handout pages into locally governed teaching assets.
Preserve source URL, local path, caption, stain, role, and case/entity linkage for every image.

teachingMechanisms.label: Image first, answer second
learnerFlow: Display only the image and clinical header.
Require the learner to state the compartment, process, and immediate differential.
Reveal the microscopic findings only after an observation is recorded.
Reveal ancillary studies only after morphology-driven reasoning is complete.
Reveal final diagnosis and report language last.

teachingMechanisms.label: Diagnostic commitment
learnerFlow: Learner enters diagnosis, grade/classification, and one exclusion.
Faculty reveals the strongest confirming feature and the highest-risk mimic.
Learner revises the report before the answer key is shown.

teachingMechanisms.label: Sign-out efficiency
learnerFlow: Learner identifies the report-critical elements in under 90 seconds.
Learner dictates a concise diagnosis line.
Learner checks whether radiology, margins, biomarkers, and staging are adequately addressed.

teachingMechanisms.label: Synoptic build
learnerFlow: Learner completes structured fields from image and scenario data.
Faculty compares the draft with the locked expected report.

cases.title: Benign calcifications on stereotactic core
cases.diagnosticTask: Explain benign calcifications on core biopsy and avoid overcalling usual ductal hyperplasia as atypia.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Name the process explaining the targeted calcifications.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Calcifications are present in benign ducts/lobules with fibrocystic change and no atypical epithelial proliferation.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Exclude the lesion that would change management.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: No monomorphic low-grade proliferation meeting ADH or DCIS criteria.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Dictate the final core biopsy diagnosis.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Benign breast tissue with fibrocystic change and benign calcifications; negative for atypia and malignancy.

cases.reportingTarget: Benign breast tissue with fibrocystic change and benign calcifications; negative for atypia and malignancy.
pitfalls: Failure to state whether calcifications are explained
Overcalling florid usual ductal hyperplasia as ADH

cases.title: Atypical ductal hyperplasia on core biopsy
cases.diagnosticTask: Separate ADH from low-grade DCIS using morphology plus extent in core biopsy material.

requiredCaptionElements: CK5/6 reduced or absent in atypical focus

diagnosticSteps.prompt: State whether the proliferation is usual or atypical.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: The proliferation is monomorphic with rigid spaces, supporting atypical ductal proliferation.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: State why the diagnosis is not DCIS on this core.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: The atypical proliferation is quantitatively limited in the sampled tissue.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: State the management-facing diagnosis for the report.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Atypical ductal hyperplasia with calcifications, requiring radiology-pathology concordance and usual clinical consideration of excision.

pitfalls: Using immunostains instead of extent to separate ADH from low-grade DCIS
Ignoring radiology-pathology concordance

cases.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ on core biopsy
cases.diagnosticTask: Report DCIS grade, architecture, necrosis, calcifications, and absence of invasion.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Determine whether the lesion is in situ or invasive.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: The malignant epithelial proliferation remains within ducts with retained myoepithelial cells.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: List the report-required DCIS qualifiers.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Nuclear grade, architectural pattern, necrosis, calcifications, and no invasive carcinoma identified.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Dictate the DCIS biopsy sign-out line.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Ductal carcinoma in situ, nuclear grade 2, cribriform and solid patterns, with necrosis and calcifications; no invasive carcinoma identified.

cases.reportingTarget: Ductal carcinoma in situ, nuclear grade 2, cribriform and solid patterns, with necrosis and calcifications; no invasive carcinoma identified.
pitfalls: Omitting necrosis or calcifications
Calling DCIS invasive without stromal invasion

cases.title: Invasive carcinoma of no special type on core biopsy
cases.diagnosticTask: Recognize invasive NST carcinoma and translate biomarkers into clinically useful receptor class.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Identify invasion and the special type status.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Infiltrative malignant glands/nests in desmoplastic stroma support invasive carcinoma NST.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Convert biomarker values to receptor class.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: ER and PR positive with HER2 1+ is hormone receptor positive and HER2 negative.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: State the biomarker reporting consequence.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: The carcinoma is hormone receptor positive and HER2 negative; HER2 1+ is reported as negative by IHC.

cases.reportingTarget: Invasive carcinoma of no special type, provisional Nottingham grade as appropriate on core biopsy; biomarkers reported separately.
pitfalls: Treating HER2 1+ as HER2 positive
Omitting associated DCIS in the diagnostic comment

cases.title: Classic invasive lobular carcinoma
cases.diagnosticTask: Recognize discohesive single-file and targetoid growth and use E-cadherin/p120 appropriately.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Name the invasive pattern.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Discohesive cells infiltrate in single files and targetoid arrays.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: State the ancillary result that supports lobular phenotype.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Loss of membranous E-cadherin supports lobular carcinoma in the correct morphologic context.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Dictate the diagnostic line and ancillary support.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Invasive lobular carcinoma, classic type; loss of membranous E-cadherin supports lobular phenotype.

pitfalls: Underestimating extent
Using E-cadherin without first recognizing the growth pattern

cases.title: Lumpectomy synoptic margins
cases.diagnosticTask: Separately report invasive carcinoma and DCIS margins when both components are present.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Separate invasive and DCIS margin statements.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Invasive carcinoma and DCIS are measured and reported as separate margin fields.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: State the closest negative margin for each component.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Posterior margin to invasive carcinoma is 3 mm; superior margin to DCIS is 2 mm.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Dictate the separate margin sentence.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Margins are negative; closest invasive carcinoma margin is posterior at 3 mm, and closest DCIS margin is superior at 2 mm.

cases.reportingTarget: Margins negative; closest invasive carcinoma margin 3 mm posterior; closest DCIS margin 2 mm superior.
pitfalls: Reporting only one closest margin
Mixing invasive and DCIS margin distances

cases.title: ER low positive biomarker reporting
cases.diagnosticTask: Classify 1-10% ER nuclear staining as ER low positive and state intensity.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Estimate percentage and intensity of ER nuclear staining.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Approximately 5% of invasive tumor nuclei show weak ER staining.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Translate the result into the required reporting category.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: ER low positive.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Dictate the ER result exactly.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: ER low positive, weak intensity, approximately 5% of invasive tumor nuclei.

cases.reportingTarget: ER low positive, weak intensity, approximately 5% of invasive tumor nuclei.
pitfalls: Calling ER low positive negative
Omitting the low-positive qualifier

cases.title: Post-neoadjuvant breast resection
cases.diagnosticTask: Use yp staging and separately document residual tumor, tumor bed, and treatment effect in breast and nodes.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Distinguish tumor bed size from residual invasive tumor size.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Tumor bed is 3.2 cm; residual invasive carcinoma is 6 mm.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Apply post-treatment staging convention.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Use ypTNM and document treatment effect in breast and nodes.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Dictate the post-treatment staging and reporting sentence.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Post-neoadjuvant specimen with 6 mm residual invasive carcinoma in a 3.2 cm tumor bed and one node with a 1.5 mm residual metastasis; stage with y prefix.

cases.reportingTarget: Post-neoadjuvant specimen with 6 mm residual invasive carcinoma in a 3.2 cm tumor bed; one node with 1.5 mm residual metastasis; stage with y prefix.
pitfalls: Staging tumor bed as residual invasive size
Failing to document nodal treatment effect

cases.title: Mucinous carcinoma receptor summary
cases.diagnosticTask: Recognize mucinous carcinoma and summarize biomarkers as hormone receptor positive and HER2 negative.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Name the special type.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Floating clusters of carcinoma in extracellular mucin support mucinous carcinoma.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Summarize receptor class.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Hormone receptor positive and HER2 negative.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Dictate the receptor-class sentence.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Invasive mucinous carcinoma; hormone receptor positive and HER2 negative.

cases.reportingTarget: Invasive mucinous carcinoma; hormone receptor positive and HER2 negative.
pitfalls: Failing to distinguish pure from mixed mucinous carcinoma
Overcomplicating receptor summary

cases.title: HER2 IHC score 0
cases.diagnosticTask: Distinguish HER2 score 0 from 1+ using membrane staining extent and completeness.

requiredCaptionElements: less than or equal to 10% of cells

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Describe membrane staining.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Faint incomplete membrane staining is present in no more than 10% of tumor cells.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Assign HER2 score.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: HER2 IHC score 0.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Dictate the HER2 result exactly.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: HER2 IHC score 0 because faint incomplete membrane staining is present in no more than 10% of tumor cells.

pitfalls: Inflating minimal incomplete staining to 1+
Ignoring percentage cutoff

cases.title: Lumpectomy with sentinel lymph nodes
cases.specimenType: Partial mastectomy with separately submitted sentinel lymph nodes
cases.diagnosticTask: Complete a CAP-style invasive carcinoma resection sign-out with separate invasive carcinoma, DCIS, margin, lymphovascular invasion, sentinel node, biomarker, and pTNM elements.
cases.grossScenario: Left partial mastectomy contains a 2.1 cm firm irregular mass with adjacent calcifications. Three sentinel lymph nodes are submitted separately. Invasive carcinoma is 4 mm from the anterior margin and DCIS is 1.5 mm from the superior margin.

capProtocol.versionAnchor: CAP Breast Invasive Resection v4.9.0.1 or current institutional CAP protocol

fields: extent if required by local practice
invasive carcinoma margin status and closest distance
DCIS margin status and closest distance
biomarker block or prior biomarker reference

capProtocol.stagingNotes: Use pTNM for upfront surgery. Do not combine DCIS and invasive carcinoma margin distances into a single closest margin statement.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: State the invasive carcinoma type, size, grade, and lymphovascular invasion status.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: The report begins with invasive carcinoma NST, tumor size 2.1 cm, Nottingham grade, and LVI status.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Separate invasive carcinoma margin reporting from DCIS margin reporting.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Closest invasive carcinoma margin is anterior at 4 mm; closest DCIS margin is superior at 1.5 mm.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Complete sentinel node accounting.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Total nodes, sentinel nodes, positive node category, size of largest deposit, and extranodal extension are all explicitly addressed.

items: separate additional margin specimens if submitted

cases.reportingTarget: Invasive carcinoma of no special type, 2.1 cm, Nottingham grade as assigned, with associated DCIS; margins negative with invasive carcinoma 4 mm from anterior margin and DCIS 1.5 mm from superior margin; sentinel lymph nodes reported with total count, positive count, largest deposit, extranodal extension, and pTNM.
pitfalls: Reporting only one closest margin when invasive carcinoma and DCIS are both present
Failing to state sentinel node denominator
Omitting LVI

cases.title: Mastectomy for extensive DCIS with sentinel node sampling
cases.specimenType: Skin-sparing mastectomy with nipple margin and sentinel lymph node biopsy
cases.diagnosticTask: Complete a DCIS resection sign-out including extent, grade, necrosis, margins, nipple/skin involvement, sentinel node accounting, and pTis staging.
cases.grossScenario: Right skin-sparing mastectomy for extensive calcifications. DCIS spans 6.4 cm across sequential slices. Nipple margin is submitted separately. Two sentinel lymph nodes are negative.

capProtocol.versionAnchor: CAP Breast DCIS Resection v4.4.0.0 or current institutional CAP protocol
capProtocol.stagingNotes: Use the DCIS resection protocol when there is no invasive carcinoma. If invasion or microinvasion is identified, move to the invasive carcinoma protocol.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Confirm whether the case belongs in the DCIS or invasive carcinoma protocol.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: No invasive carcinoma or microinvasion is identified, so the DCIS resection protocol applies.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: State how extent is derived.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: DCIS extent is estimated from the distribution across sequential gross slices and histologic confirmation.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Account for sentinel lymph nodes without implying invasive carcinoma.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Two sentinel nodes are examined and negative; final tumor stage remains pTis (DCIS).

cases.reportingTarget: Ductal carcinoma in situ, estimated extent 6.4 cm, grade and necrosis as observed, margins negative with closest margin specified, sentinel lymph nodes 0/2, pTis (DCIS) pN0 if applicable.
pitfalls: Using the invasive carcinoma protocol when no invasion is present
Omitting extent in mastectomy DCIS
Treating sentinel node sampling as evidence of invasion

cases.title: Post-neoadjuvant lumpectomy with sentinel nodes
cases.specimenType: Partial mastectomy after neoadjuvant therapy with sentinel lymph node biopsy
cases.diagnosticTask: Separate tumor bed, residual invasive carcinoma, residual cellularity, DCIS, nodal response, and ypTNM in a treated breast resection.
cases.grossScenario: Tumor bed measures 3.8 cm. Residual invasive carcinoma is present as scattered foci spanning 9 mm within the bed. Residual cellularity is low. Residual DCIS is present. Two sentinel lymph nodes show treatment effect; one contains a 1.2 mm residual metastasis.

capProtocol.protocol: Breast invasive carcinoma resection after neoadjuvant therapy
capProtocol.versionAnchor: Current CAP invasive breast resection protocol with post-treatment reporting fields

fields: residual cancer burden inputs if institution uses RCB

capProtocol.stagingNotes: Use ypTNM after neoadjuvant therapy. Do not stage the full tumor bed as residual invasive carcinoma.

treatmentResponse.context: Neoadjuvant systemic therapy before breast-conserving surgery
treatmentResponse.teachingNote: The sign-out should make treatment response auditable by tumor board; the learner must distinguish tumor bed from viable invasive carcinoma.

lymphNodeWorkup.nodalResponse: Treatment effect present in nodes; one node contains micrometastatic residual disease.
lymphNodeWorkup.stagingFocus: ypN category is based on residual nodal disease after therapy.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: State tumor bed size separately from residual invasive tumor size.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Tumor bed is 3.8 cm; residual invasive carcinoma spans 9 mm.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: List the required breast treatment response elements.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Residual invasive carcinoma, residual DCIS, treatment effect, and residual cellularity are all documented.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Complete nodal response accounting.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Two sentinel nodes examined; one has a 1.2 mm residual metastasis with treatment effect; ypN category follows residual disease.

cases.reportingTarget: Post-neoadjuvant partial mastectomy with 9 mm residual invasive carcinoma in a 3.8 cm tumor bed, residual DCIS present, treatment effect in breast and nodes, one sentinel node with 1.2 mm residual metastasis, ypTNM assigned from residual disease.
pitfalls: Using tumor bed size as residual invasive size
Omitting nodal treatment effect
Using pTNM rather than ypTNM

cases.title: Post-neoadjuvant mastectomy with no lymph nodes submitted
cases.specimenType: Total mastectomy after neoadjuvant therapy, no lymph nodes submitted
cases.diagnosticTask: Sign out a treated mastectomy with no residual invasive carcinoma and no lymph nodes, while preserving tumor bed documentation and staging limitations.
cases.grossScenario: Mastectomy shows a 2.6 cm fibrotic tumor bed with biopsy clip. Entire tumor bed is submitted. No residual invasive carcinoma or DCIS is identified. No lymph nodes are submitted or found.

capProtocol.protocol: Post-neoadjuvant breast resection with no residual carcinoma
capProtocol.versionAnchor: Current CAP invasive breast resection protocol plus institutional handling for no residual cancer after neoadjuvant therapy

fields: no lymph nodes submitted or found
ypN cannot be assigned from this specimen

capProtocol.stagingNotes: A complete response in the breast still requires documentation of tumor bed and sampling. Nodal status cannot be inferred when no nodes are submitted.

treatmentResponse.context: Mastectomy after neoadjuvant therapy with pathologic complete response in breast
treatmentResponse.teachingNote: The learner must state what is known from the specimen and what cannot be assigned because no nodes were submitted.

lymphNodeWorkup.submitted: No lymph nodes submitted or found
lymphNodeWorkup.nodalResponse: Cannot assess nodal response in this specimen.
lymphNodeWorkup.stagingFocus: ypNX if no regional lymph nodes are submitted or found.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Confirm whether residual invasive carcinoma or DCIS is present.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: No residual invasive carcinoma or DCIS is identified in the submitted tumor bed.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: State the tumor bed and sampling evidence.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Tumor bed is 2.6 cm with biopsy clip correlation and complete histologic submission.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: State the nodal staging limitation.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: No nodes are submitted; nodal response and ypN status cannot be assessed from this specimen.

items: do not imply nodal complete response

cases.reportingTarget: Post-neoadjuvant mastectomy with treatment effect/tumor bed measuring 2.6 cm and no residual invasive carcinoma or DCIS identified; no lymph nodes submitted or found; ypT0 ypNX if supported by prior invasive carcinoma diagnosis and institutional staging practice.
pitfalls: Calling nodal pathologic complete response without nodes
Omitting tumor bed size
Failing to reference the prior diagnostic biopsy

cases.title: Mastectomy with immediate reconstruction and implant capsule pathology
cases.specimenType: Completion mastectomy with immediate reconstruction, acellular dermal matrix, implant capsule, and additional skin
cases.diagnosticTask: Separate oncologic resection reporting from reconstruction-associated specimens, implant capsule findings, foreign-body reaction, and lymphoma exclusion when clinically indicated.
cases.grossScenario: Completion mastectomy shows no residual invasive carcinoma at the prior lumpectomy site. A fibrous implant capsule and acellular dermal matrix are submitted from reconstruction. Capsule shows dense fibrosis, synovial-like metaplasia, chronic inflammation, and foreign-body giant cell reaction. No atypical lymphoid infiltrate is identified.

capProtocol.protocol: Breast resection plus non-neoplastic implant/reconstruction specimens
capProtocol.versionAnchor: Use the applicable CAP breast cancer protocol for the oncologic specimen; implant capsule findings are reported descriptively unless lymphoma or another neoplasm is identified.

fields: CD30/ALK workup if atypical lymphoid cells are present

capProtocol.stagingNotes: Do not force benign implant capsule findings into the carcinoma synoptic. Report them in a separate diagnosis/comment section.

reconstructionImplant.scenario: Immediate reconstruction with implant capsule and acellular dermal matrix submitted with oncologic specimen.
pathologyTasks: document no residual carcinoma if true
separate benign reconstruction findings from carcinoma fields
implantCapsuleTasks: screen for atypical CD30-positive lymphoid infiltrate when clinical setting raises concern
reconstructionImplant.reportingBoundary: The carcinoma synoptic covers the breast cancer specimen; implant capsule pathology is a separate descriptive diagnosis unless a neoplasm is present.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Separate the oncologic specimen from reconstruction-associated tissue.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: No residual carcinoma is reported separately from implant capsule and acellular dermal matrix findings.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Describe the implant capsule pathology.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Fibrous capsule with synovial-like metaplasia, chronic inflammation, and foreign-body giant cell reaction.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: State when BIA-ALCL workup is required.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Late seroma, mass, or atypical lymphoid infiltrate should trigger cytology/histology correlation and CD30-based workup with ALK as appropriate.

items: atypical lymphoid infiltrate absent or present

cases.reportingTarget: Breast, completion mastectomy: prior procedure site changes, no residual carcinoma identified. Implant capsule/acellular dermal matrix: fibrous capsule with synovial-like metaplasia, chronic inflammation, and foreign-body giant cell reaction; no atypical lymphoid infiltrate identified.
pitfalls: Mixing benign implant capsule findings into carcinoma stage fields
Ignoring late seroma or mass history
Ordering CD30 indiscriminately without a clinical or morphologic trigger

cases.title: Late peri-implant seroma with capsule excision
cases.specimenType: Peri-implant seroma fluid, implant capsule, and regional lymph node excision
cases.diagnosticTask: Work up implant-associated seroma/capsule specimens for silicone reaction versus breast implant-associated anaplastic large cell lymphoma, including CD30, ALK, capsule invasion, and nodal assessment.
cases.grossScenario: Patient with textured implant placed 9 years earlier presents with delayed peri-implant seroma and capsular thickening. Seroma cytology contains atypical large lymphoid cells. Capsule excision shows fibrinous material and scattered atypical CD30-positive cells confined to the luminal surface. ALK is negative. One excised lymph node is negative.

capProtocol.protocol: Implant-associated lymphoma workup with separate breast cancer protocol only if carcinoma is present
capProtocol.versionAnchor: Use current hematopathology/WHO-aligned lymphoma reporting practice; CAP breast carcinoma synoptic is not the correct template unless carcinoma is also present.
capProtocol.stagingNotes: This is not staged as breast carcinoma. The report should clearly define compartment and extent for surgical/oncologic management.

reconstructionImplant.scenario: Late peri-implant seroma with capsular thickening and atypical lymphoid cells.
pathologyTasks: triage seroma fluid for cytology/cell block
correlate with implant history and imaging
implantCapsuleTasks: determine whether atypical cells are luminal only or invading capsule
reconstructionImplant.reportingBoundary: Do not use a breast carcinoma synoptic. Use lymphoma-oriented diagnostic language and extent descriptors.

requiredCounts: nodes involved by lymphoma if present
lymphNodeWorkup.nodalResponse: Not a neoadjuvant carcinoma response case.
lymphNodeWorkup.stagingFocus: Node involvement changes implant-associated lymphoma extent rather than breast carcinoma pN stage.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: State the clinical trigger for implant-associated lymphoma workup.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Delayed peri-implant seroma with capsular thickening years after implant placement is the trigger.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Classify the compartment of disease.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: Atypical CD30-positive ALK-negative cells are confined to the seroma/capsule luminal surface without capsule invasion in this teaching scenario.

diagnosticSteps.prompt: Account for lymph nodes without using breast carcinoma pN language.
diagnosticSteps.expectedObservation: One lymph node is examined and negative for lymphoma; this is reported as lymphoma extent, not carcinoma pN staging.

cases.reportingTarget: Peri-implant seroma/capsule: CD30-positive ALK-negative atypical T-cell proliferation consistent with breast implant-associated anaplastic large cell lymphoma in the appropriate clinical setting, confined to seroma/capsule luminal surface in this scenario; lymph node negative if confirmed.
pitfalls: Using breast carcinoma CAP synoptic for lymphoma
Failing to sample a capsule mass
Missing silicone granuloma as a mimic
Not documenting whether capsule invasion is present

Ductal Carcinoma In Situ (DCIS): p63 or smooth muscle myosin if invasion is questioned
Flat Epithelial Atypia (FEA): ER optional, CK5/6 optional in difficult cases
Paget Disease of Nipple: p63 to exclude squamous mimics if needed
Secretory Carcinoma: mammaglobin, SOX10, pan-TRK or molecular confirmation as available
Metaplastic Carcinoma: p63 or p40 as clinically needed

acceptanceCriteria: Every case has at least one diagnostic image requirement before it can be released.
Every entity has at least one H&E diagnostic histology slot in the source manifest.
Every acquired image has a local file path, source URL or source document path, caption, stain or marker, and case/entity linkage.
Every case has image-first prompts, delayed reveal content, final report language, and pitfalls.
The validation script passes in non-strict mode and reports unresolved source slots separately from schema failures.
Strict mode may be enabled when the lecture is ready for release and should fail on any missing image source or local file.
